text:
Dear friend,

I heard about the exam, and I want you to know that I have been thinking of you. A failed exam feels, in the moment, like a verdict on something larger than itself, as though the whole of your effort and even your worth had been weighed and found wanting. But it is not that. It is one measurement, taken on one difficult day, of a small slice of who you are.

I have always believed that the people worth admiring are not those who never stumble, but those who learn to rise again with their dignity intact. You studied. You showed up. You tried something hard, and trying hard is never the same as failing, no matter what the score insists.

Give yourself a little while to feel the disappointment honestly; it deserves that much. Then, when you are ready, look at what this exam taught you, not about your limits, but about your method, your pacing, the places where you can grow. Failure, handled gently, becomes a kind of instruction.

You are more capable than this single result can show. I have seen your persistence, and I trust it. This is a setback, not an ending, and I will be cheering for you when you walk back in to try again.

With great affection and unwavering faith in you, always.
